basic_details.job_title: WII Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 20 Project Scientist, Project Associate and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: Wildlife Institute of India (WII) Project Positions Notification 2026 has been released on 07 May 2026 on the official website www.wii.gov.in . A total of 20 vacancies have been announced through the WII Project Positions Recruitment 2026. Candidates (age details as per official notification, with relaxations for SC/ST/OBC) who have qualifications such as PhD, Master’s in Natural Sciences/Wildlife Sciences/Ecology/Forestry, M.Tech/B.Tech in CS/IT/RS-GIS/AI/Electronics, or MVSc/BVSc are eligible to apply for these Project Positions vacancies. The online application process for WII Project Posit

age_limit.details: All Posts: Age relaxation is applicable up to 5 years for SC/ST and up to 3 years for OBC candidates as per GoI norms.

qualification.education_qualification: PhD in Natural Sciences, Master’s in Natural Sciences / Wildlife Sciences / Ecology / Forestry, M.Tech / B.Tech in CS/IT/RS-GIS/AI/Electronics, MVSc / BVSc, NET/GATE qualified (preferred for higher pay)
qualification.details: Project Manager (Training & Capacity): PhD in Natural Sciences / M.Tech (CS/IT/RS-GIS/AI etc.) / MVSc + >=5 years training & capacity building exp. Project Scientist – I (Ecology): PhD Natural Sciences / M.Tech (CS/IT/RS-GIS/AI etc.) / MVSc Project Scientist – I (Geoinformatics): PhD Natural Sciences / M.Tech (CS/IT/RS-GIS/AI/Electronics etc.) / MVSc Principal Project Associate (Aquatic Ecology): Master’s + 6-8 yrs exp. / PhD + 4 yrs exp. in relevant fields Senior Project Associate (Ecotoxicology): Master’s + 2-4 yrs / PhD in relevant fields Project Associate-II (Ecology): Master’s / 4-yr BSc + 2 yrs exp. / BVSc + 2 yrs (NET/GATE preferred) Project Associate-II (Ecotoxicology): Master’s / 4-yr BSc + 2 yrs exp. / BVSc + 2 yrs (NET/GATE preferred) Project Associate-I: Master’s / 4-yr BSc / B.Tech / BVSc (NET/GATE preferred) Project Associate-I (Ecotoxicology): Master’s / 4-yr BSc / B.Tech / BVSc (NET/GATE preferred) Project Associate-I (Global Best Standards): Master’s in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences Project Associate-II (Global Best Standards): Master’s + 2 years R&D experience Project Scientist – I (Kanger Valley National Park): PhD in Natural Sciences / Forestry Sciences Project Associate-I (Kanger Valley National Park): Master’s in Natural Sciences / Agricultural Sciences Project Scientist-I (Select Habitat Monitoring): PhD Natural Sciences / Master’s in Engineering/Technology

vacancy_details.Post Name: Project Scientist – I (Kanger Valley National Park)

selection_process: Screening / Shortlisting Online Interview Document Verification
how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the WII Various Posts Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 22-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of WII to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for WII Project Positions?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for the WII Project Positions is 22 May 2026. Ensure your application is submitted well before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for WII Project Positions?
faq.answer: There are a total of 20 vacancies for various Project Positions announced by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) in this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for General/OBC candidates?
faq.answer: The application fee for General and OBC category candidates is Rs. 500/-. SC/ST candidates are required to pay Rs. 100/-.

faq.question: Q4. What is the highest salary offered for WII Project Positions?
faq.answer: The highest salary offered for the Project Manager (Training & Capacity) post is Rs. 1,20,000/- consolidated per month.

faq.question: Q5. What is the minimum qualification required for Project Associate-I posts?
faq.answer: For Project Associate-I posts, the minimum qualification is a Master’s degree, a 4-year BSc, B.Tech, or BVSc. NET/GATE qualification is preferred.

faq.question: Q6. Can candidates with a BVSc degree apply for these positions?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates with a BVSc degree are eligible for certain Project Associate positions, provided they meet the additional experience requirements where specified.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided as per Government of India norms, with up to 5 years for SC/ST candidates and up to 3 years for OBC candidates.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for WII Project Positions?
faq.answer: The selection process typically involves screening/shortlisting of applications, followed by an online interview and document verification.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be accessed through the 'Important Links' section of this article. It is highly recommended to read it thoroughly.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website of the Wildlife Institute of India?
faq.answer: The official website of the Wildlife Institute of India is https://wii.gov.in. You can visit this site for more information and updates.
